title: parsonsia bustamanta
side_effects: Parsonsia bustamanta can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, vomiting, and dizziness. Prolonged consumption of the plant may also lead to headaches, fatigue, and an increased risk of bleeding due to its anticoagulant properties. In severe cases, overuse of Parsonsia bustamanta can cause respiratory issues, such as shortness of breath and coughing, as well as gastrointestinal problems like diarrhea and abdominal pain. If not handled correctly, the plant's toxic compounds can also lead to seizures and other serious neurological symptoms.
